title: inspect.go: inspect env variables
body:
Update the inspect command to show the environment variables

Signed-off-by: José Guilherme Vanz <jguilhermevanz@suse.com>

This change requires a change in the `containers/image` library: https://github.com/containers/image/pull/652

comments.body:
The expected output using the branch from the `containers/image` PR is:

```
jvanz@earth:~/go/src/github.com/containers/skopeo> make binary-local && ./skopeo inspect docker://docker.io/fedora
go build "-buildmode=pie" -ldflags "-X main.gitCommit=d470b4d03f918da8a9fbaecc355e61a260186c85" -gcflags "" -tags "   containers_image_ostree_stub " -o skopeo ./cmd/skopeo
go: finding github.com/jvanz/image 248
{
    "Name": "docker.io/library/fedora",
    "Digest": "sha256:9c78c69f748953ba8fdb6eb9982e1abefe281d9b931a13f251eb8aec988353de",
    "RepoTags": [
        "20",
        "21",
        "22",
        "23",
        "24",
        "25",
        "26-modular",
        "26",
        "27",
        "28",
        "29",
        "30",
        "31",
        "branched",
        "heisenbug",
        "latest",
        "modular",
        "rawhide"
    ],
    "Created": "2019-06-10T23:20:17.083110434Z",
    "DockerVersion": "18.06.1-ce",
    "Labels": {
        "maintainer": "Clement Verna \u003ccverna@fedoraproject.org\u003e"
    },
    "Architecture": "amd64",
    "Os": "linux",
    "Layers": [
        "sha256:8f6ac7ed4a91c9630083524efcef2f59f27404320bfee44397f544c252ad4bd4"
    ],
    "Env": [
        "PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin",
        "DISTTAG=f30-updates-candidatecontainer",
        "FGC=f30-updates-candidate",
        "FBR=f30-updates-candidate"
    ]
}
```
Tests are not yet happy `cmd/skopeo/inspect.go:146:28: imgInspect.Env undefined (type *types.ImageInspectInfo has no field or method Env)` and we need to rebase.

> The changes here a good, but for testing the PR over c/image, we need to vendor those changes. You can do that via `go mod edit -replace github.com/containers/image=$yourRepository@$yourBranch`, then do a `make vendor` and commit/push the changes here.

I see. I thought I could not commit vendor changes. Let me ask you something, how do you, in the maintainer side, avoid merging my repository as module vendor ? Do you choose manually which changes will be merged into the `master` branch?

> > The changes here a good, but for testing the PR over c/image, we need to vendor those changes.

We don’t AFAICS; the c/image PR is backwards-compatible, and can be tested/merged without involving skopeo at all.

> > You can do that via `go mod edit -replace github.com/containers/image=$yourRepository@$yourBranch`, then do a `make vendor` and commit/push the changes here.
> 
> I see. I thought I could not commit vendor changes.

Yeah, vendoring from forks of c/image is sometimes necessary for tests ( https://github.com/containers/skopeo/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#dependencies-management ) but we don’t want to merge that in skopeo.

> Let me ask you something, how do you, in the maintainer side, avoid merging my repository as module vendor ? Do you choose manually which changes will be merged into the `master` branch?

A big DO NOT MERGE in the PR title, like the one I have just added, typically helps :)
@jvanz Are you still working on this one?
Not now. Don't I need to wait until the requested change in the containers/image get released to merge this PR?

> Not now. Don't I need to wait until the requested change in the containers/image get released to merge this PR?

It has been merged in c/image and is part of the latest release (https://github.com/containers/image/releases/tag/v2.0.1). However, as Skopeo is used to test c/image, we can vendor in non-release commits as well. Just for Skopeo releases, we want to have a c/image release vendored.

> > Not now. Don't I need to wait until the requested change in the containers/image get released to merge this PR?
> 
> It has been merged in c/image and is part of the latest release (https://github.com/containers/image/releases/tag/v2.0.1). However, as Skopeo is used to test c/image, we can vendor in non-release commits as well. Just for Skopeo releases, we want to have a c/image release vendored.

I see. I'll update the vendor asap.
CI is happy. I think this PR can be merged. :)
